title: gladiolus permeabilis subsp. edulis
intro: Gladiolus permeabilis subsp. edulis, also known as the edible gladiolus or wild gladiolus, is a plant species that belongs to the iris family and is native to the mountainous regions of the Mediterranean. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its roots, which contain compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Consuming the plant has been associated with various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. Gladiolus permeabilis subsp. edulis can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
